risks.risk: Core ledger API not ready by project kick-off
risks.description: If the core ledger API event streaming capability is delayed beyond Q2 2026, the reconciliation engine cannot be built against a live endpoint, forcing a rework of the integration layer.
risks.mitigation: Implement fallback processing with a scheduled batch pull adapter as a temporary bridge; engage IT Architecture monthly for API readiness tracking; escalation path to CTO if delayed past 30 June 2026.

risks.risk: Vendor delays to payments gateway unified ID delivery
risks.description: The payments gateway vendor's ID schema update is on their roadmap but not contractually committed; slippage would break the automated matching logic.
risks.mitigation: Negotiate SLA commitment with the vendor as part of the next contract renewal; develop a fuzzy-matching fallback using amount, date, and counterparty as composite key; track vendor milestone monthly.

risks.risk: Operations team resistance to new workflow
risks.description: Analysts accustomed to spreadsheet-based reconciliation may resist adopting the new exception queue interface, leading to shadow processes that undermine the audit trail.
risks.mitigation: Engage operations team as co-designers during UAT; run parallel processing for 4 weeks post go-live so analysts retain a safety net; provide structured training and a feedback channel.

risks.risk: Regulatory deadline missed due to scope creep
risks.description: Additional requirements added mid-delivery could push go-live past the 30 September 2026 regulatory reporting deadline.
risks.mitigation: Strict change control board with fortnightly reviews; any change request assessed for timeline impact before approval; Phase 2 backlog maintained for post-deadline enhancements.
